agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: New Burnaby Sign Bylaw and enforcement amendments
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed new Sign Bylaw that would replace the current rules for all signs on commercial and residential properties. The proposal also includes changes to the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw, which is the system that handles fines and penalties for breaking city rules. If approved, this would affect how businesses display signs, including size, placement, and lighting. The new bylaw has not been passed yet — Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the proposed sign rules — they could affect your business signage.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
PROPOSED NEW BURNABY SIGN BYLAW
Purpose:
To seek Council approval for the proposed new Sign Bylaw and related amendments to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Strata title conversion for 4558 Portland Street duplex
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to allow the owner of an existing two-family dwelling at 4558 Portland Street to convert it into strata lots. This means the building would be split into separate, individually owned units (like condos) instead of being owned as one single property. The decision is not final — Council will vote on whether to approve this conversion at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time — this is a site-specific application that does not affect other properties.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
STRATA TITLE CONVERSION APPLICATION #25-02 - 4558 PORTLAND STREET
Purpose: To obtain Council authority for strata titling of an existing occupied two-family dwelling.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Time-limited changes to Burnaby rental housing rules for developers
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will vote on temporary amendments to the Rental Use Zoning Policy (Stream 2) for eligible RM District developments. For pre-construction projects, the inclusionary rental requirement would be calculated from the strata component only, and the required below-market rate would change from 20% below CMHC median rents to Burnaby-wide CMHC median market rents. For under-construction projects, the same rental-rate change applies. Eligibility deadlines: pre-construction projects must obtain a building permit by June 30, 2027 and commence construction by December 31, 2027; under-construction projects must commence or complete construction by December 31, 2027.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the June 9, 2026 council meeting and consider submitting comments if you are a developer with an eligible RM District project.

key_dates.label: Council vote on rental rule amendments — Burnaby
Building permit deadline for pre-construction projects — Burnaby RUZP

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
PROPOSED TIME LIMITED AMENDMENTS TO THE INCLUSIONARY RENTAL REQUIREMENTS OF THE RENTAL USE ZONING POLICY FOR ELIGIBLE DEVELOPMENTS
Purpose:
To seek Council approval of time-limited amendments to the inclusionary rental requirements (Stream 2) of the Rental Use Zoning Policy for eligible pre-construction and under-construction developments to encourage new housing supply.

attachments.title: Proposed Time Limited Amendments to the Inclusionary Rental Requirements of the

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on buying two aerial ladder fire trucks
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award to purchase two new aerial ladder fire trucks. These are the large fire trucks with extendable ladders used to fight fires in tall buildings. If approved, the contract would authorize the city to buy the trucks for the fire department. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be decided at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - TWO AERIAL LADDER FIRE TRUCKS
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the purchase of two (2) aerial ladder fire trucks.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Kensington Park playground contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and installation of new playground structures at Kensington Park. If approved, the contract would go to a chosen company to build the playground. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be decided at the upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - KENSINGTON PARK SUPPLY INSTALL OF PLAYGROUND STRUCTURES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Kensington Park – Supply and Install of Playground Structure.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on security system contract renewal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract renewal for the city's Security Incident Detection System. This system is used to monitor and detect security incidents in city facilities. If approved, the renewal would extend the existing contract for this technology. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CR - SECURITY INCIDENT DETECTION SYSTEM - RENEWAL
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Security Incident Detection System.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing contract for holiday lights supply and maintenance
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed contract renewal for the supply, installation, maintenance, removal, and storage of LED holiday lights. If approved, this would extend the current arrangement for decorating public spaces during the holiday season. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting — it has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
CR - HOLIDAY LIGHTS (LED)
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Supply, Install, Maintenance, Removal and Storage of Holiday Lights.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Playground Development Grants Spring 2026 intake
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the Spring 2026 intake for the Playground Development Grant Program. If approved, this program would provide funding to help build or upgrade playgrounds in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the recommendation at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - PLAYGROUND DEVELOPMENT GRANTS 2026
Purpose:
To seek Council approval for the Playground Development Grant Program, Spring 2026 intake recommendation.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Sport Hosting Grant Spring 2026 intake approval
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the Spring 2026 intake of the Sport Hosting Grant Program. If approved, this program would provide funding to local sports organizations to help them bring sporting events to Burnaby. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, local sports groups can apply for funding to host events in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
ECC - SPORT HOSTING GRANT SPRING 2026 INTAKE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Sport Hosting Grant Program, Spring 2026 intake recommendation.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on lease extension for Pacific Assistance Dogs Society
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to amend the lease agreement with the Pacific Assistance Dogs Society (PADS) for their location at 9048 Stormont Avenue. The proposed change would extend the Transition Phase of the lease by an additional six months. This is not yet approved — Council will consider and decide on the amendment at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
ECC - LA AMENDMENT - 9048 STORMONT AVENUE
Purpose: To seek Council approval to amend the Lease Agreement (LA) with Pacific Assistance Dogs Society (PADS) to extend the Transition Phase for an additional six-month term at 9048 Stormont Avenue.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on final approval of Burnaby's 10-year parks and rec plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on the final phase of the 'Making Life Better: Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan.' This proposed 10-year plan would guide how the city builds and improves parks, community centres, and cultural spaces. Council will consider the plan at an upcoming meeting, along with feedback from recent public engagement sessions. If approved, the plan would set priorities for new facilities and upgrades across Burnaby over the next decade.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote date — if approved, this plan will shape future park and recreation projects that could affect your business location or operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PRCC - MAKING LIFE BETTER - PRC PLAN - FINAL PHASE (PHASE 4)
Purpose: To seek approval of the final Making Life Better: Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan, and to inform on the recent public engagement and revisions made to the Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider 2025 Canada Community-Building Fund update
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider an update on the 2025 Canada Community-Building Fund. This is a federal program that provides money to municipalities for infrastructure projects like roads, transit, and water systems. The update will outline how much funding Burnaby is expected to receive and how it can be spent. No final decision has been made yet — Council will review the information and may vote on how to allocate the funds at a future meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
FMC - 2025 CANADA COMMUNITY-BUILDING FUND UPDATE
Purpose: To provide an update on the Federal Canada Community Building Fund Program for 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review major civic project updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on significant civic projects in Burnaby as of May 2026. This is a proposed review of ongoing projects, not a final decision. The update will inform council about progress, timelines, and any changes to major city-building initiatives. Business owners should watch for potential impacts on construction, development, and local infrastructure that could affect their operations.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor council decisions following this update — project changes could affect your business location or supply chain.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - MAY 2026
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026 for development
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw called the 'Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026'. This proposed bylaw would set new rules for how developers must provide or pay for essential services — like roads, water pipes, sewers, and streetlights — when they build on land in Burnaby. The goal is to make sure new developments pay their fair share for the public infrastructure they need. This is a first reading, meaning it is an early step in the approval process. The bylaw has not been passed yet, and council will need to vote on it again before it becomes law.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for future council meetings and public hearings on this bylaw — it could affect your development costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14829 - Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to respect provision of works and services in respect to development of land

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a new reserve fund called the 'Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund'. This fund would hold money paid by developers instead of building certain public works (like roads or sewers) themselves. The money would then be used by the city to pay for those works. This is a first reading of the bylaw, meaning it is still a proposal and has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14830 - Burnaby Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish a Works and Services Payment-In-Lieu Reserve Fund

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby fee changes for works and services up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the fees the city charges for certain works and services. The proposed amendment to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026) would update the fees listed under the 'works and services' section. This means businesses that need city permits, inspections, or other services related to construction, development, or municipal works could see their costs go up or down. The exact fee changes are not detailed in this agenda item, but the bylaw amendment is the first step toward approving them. Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if approved, these fee changes could affect your next permit or service application.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14831 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (works & services)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026 to regulate signs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on the first reading of the Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026, a proposed bylaw that would regulate signs across the city. If approved, this bylaw could change what signs businesses can display, where they can be placed, and how they must be maintained. The decision is not final yet — Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and any public hearing dates — this could affect your business signage.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.4
#14834 - Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment on sign enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, specifically regarding signs. This bylaw sets the rules for how the city can issue tickets and fines for bylaw violations. If approved, this change would update how sign-related infractions are enforced. The amendment is at its first reading stage, meaning it has been introduced but not yet passed. A final decision has not been made.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how sign violations are ticketed and fined for your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.5
#14835 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 up for final vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would set out the rules and steps for how development applications (like building permits, rezoning, and subdivision requests) are reviewed and approved in Burnaby. If passed, it will replace the current procedures and change how developers and property owners submit and process their applications. The vote is scheduled for the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this bylaw will change how you submit development applications in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 final adoption vote

agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14824 - Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to implement procedures for review and approval of development applications

agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to the Burnaby Building Bylaw 2016. This proposed bylaw would change the rules and procedures for how development applications are processed in the city. If approved, it could affect how builders, developers, and property owners submit plans and get permits. The exact changes to the development procedures are detailed in the proposed amendment.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could change how you submit development applications in Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would change the fees and charges related to development procedures — for example, building permits, rezoning applications, or subdivision fees. If approved, the new fee schedule would take effect after the vote. The amendment is listed as item 11.2.3 on the council agenda.

agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would authorize a Housing Agreement for a non-market rental housing development at 7880 and 7885 Newcombe Street. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and the developer that locks in the building's use as affordable rental housing for a set period. This proposed bylaw would secure those rental commitments for the new development.

A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and the developer that locks in the rental rates and ensures the units stay affordable for a set period. The proposed bylaw is called the 'Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026.' If approved, it would update the current fee schedule. agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new fees will apply to your next development application.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved new Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a new Sign Bylaw for 2026. The bylaw sets new rules for signs on businesses and properties in the city. Council also approved updates to the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to support the new sign rules. The City Solicitor has been authorized to bring the final bylaw forward for adoption. Business owners should review the new rules to ensure their signs comply.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026 to ensure your business signs comply with the updated rules.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 271
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward the proposed Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026, substantially in the form set out in Attachment 1 of the report titled "Proposed New Burnaby Sign Bylaw" dated June 9, 2026; and
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, as substantially set out in Attachment 2 of the report.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved strata title conversion for 4558 Portland Street duplex
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a strata title conversion for the existing two-family dwelling at the rear of 4558 Portland Street. This means the owner of that duplex can now legally split the property into two separate strata lots (individual titles for each unit), as long as they fully meet all the city's guidelines for converting older rental duplexes into strata units. The decision is final and was passed without any opposition.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own this property, ensure you satisfy all Burnaby's conversion guidelines before proceeding with the strata registration.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 272
THAT Strata Titling of the existing occupied two-family dwelling at the rear of 4558 Portland Street be approved, as described in the report titled "Strata Title Conversion Application #25-02 – 4558 Portland Street," dated June 9, 2026, subject to complete satisfaction of the Guidelines for Conversion of Existing and Occupied Two-Family Dwellings into Strata Title Units.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved time-limited rental policy changes for Burnaby developers
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved time-limited amendments to the Inclusionary Rental Requirements of the Rental Use Zoning Policy (RUZP). These changes apply to Stream 2 of the policy for both pre-construction and under-construction projects. Eligible developments can now enter into Housing Agreements under the new terms. The amendments are meant to adjust rental requirements for a limited time to support more rental housing projects.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you are a developer with a pre-construction or under-construction project in Burnaby, review the new Stream 2 terms to see if your project qualifies for a Housing Agreement.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 273
THAT the amendments to Stream 2 of the RUZP for pre-construction projects, as outlined in Section 3.1 of the report titled "Proposed Time-Limited Amendments to the Inclusionary Rental Requirements of the Rental Use Zoning Policy for Eligible Developments," dated June 9, 2026, be approved;   
THAT the amendments to Stream 2 of the RUZP for under-construction projects, as outlined in Section 3.2 of the report, be approved; and
THAT Housing Agreements be authorized for eligible developments according to the terms outlined in Section 3.1 or 3.2, as applicable, of the report, and the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward associated Housing Agreement Bylaws.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $5M contract for two new aerial ladder fire trucks
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract with Safetek Emergency Vehicles Ltd. for two new aerial ladder fire trucks. The total cost is estimated at $5,033,920 USD, including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual goods and services delivered and the unit prices proposed. This decision is final and the contract has been awarded.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 274
THAT a contract award to Safetek Emergency Vehicles Ltd. for an estimated total cost of $5,033,920 USD including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CA – Two Aerial Ladder Fire Trucks" dated June 9, 2026, be approved; and
THAT final payment be based on the actual quantities of goods and services delivered and unit prices as proposed.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $679,362 contract for Kensington Park playground
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract award to Habitat Systems Inc. for the supply and installation of a new playground structure at Kensington Park. The contract is for an estimated amount of $679,362 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual quantities of goods and services delivered and the unit prices proposed in the report dated June 9, 2026.
minutes_briefs.source_body:

Resolution No. 2026 - 275
THAT a contract award to Habitat Systems Inc. for an estimated amount of $679,362 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CA – Kensington Park – Supply and Install of Playground Structure" dated June 9, 2026 be approved; and
THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantities of goods and services delivered and unit prices as proposed.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

The contract is for an estimated amount of $196,000 including taxes. The final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and the unit prices proposed by CDW Canada. This decision was made at the June 9, 2026 council meeting.

Resolution No. 2026 - 276
THAT a one-year contract renewal to CDW Canada Corp. for an estimated amount of $196,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled "CR – Security Incident Detection System - Renewal" dated June 9, 2026, be approved; and

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $521,243 contract renewal with Festilight Décor for holiday lights
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved a contract renewal with Festilight Décor Inc. for holiday light installation and maintenance. The contract is for an estimated cost of $521,243 including taxes. The final payment will be based on the actual number of lights and services provided, at the unit prices submitted by the company. minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $10,475 in Playground Development Grants
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved the disbursement of $10,475 in Playground Development Grants. The funds will go to community groups as outlined in the June 2, 2026 Executive Committee report. This decision is final and the grants will be paid out as planned.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 278
THAT the disbursement of the Playground Development Grants, totaling $10,475.00, as outlined in the report titled "Playground Development Grants 2026", dated June 2, 2026, of the Executive Committee of Council meeting, be approved.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $27,000 Sport Hosting Grant for Spring 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved the disbursement of up to $27,000 in Sport Hosting Grants for the Spring 2026 intake. This means local sports organizations can now apply for funding to help cover the costs of hosting sporting events in Burnaby. The decision was made at the June 2, 2026 Executive Committee meeting and passed without opposition.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If your sports organization plans to host an event in Burnaby, contact the city's recreation department to learn how to apply for this grant funding.

key_dates.label: Spring 2026 Sport Hosting Grant application window opens

minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 279
THAT the disbursement of the Sport Hosting Grant, totaling up to $27,000 for the Spring 2026 intake, as outlined in Attachment 1 of the report titled "Sport Hosting Grant Spring 2026 Intake" dated June 2, 2026, of the Executive Committee of Council meeting, be approved.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved below-market lease extension for PADS at 9048 Stormont Avenue
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to extend the below-market rental rate of $1,000 per month (plus GST) for PADS at 9048 Stormont Avenue for an additional six months, from July 1, 2026 to December 31, 2026. This means the tenant will continue paying a reduced rent that is below the usual market rate for that property.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No action required from you — this decision is final and only affects the specific tenant at 9048 Stormont Avenue.

key_dates.label: Below-market lease extension for PADS takes effect
Below-market lease extension for PADS expires

minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 280
THAT the Lands and Facilities Department be authorized to amend the Lease Agreement with PADS at 9048 Stormont Avenue to extend the below-market rental rate of $1,000 per month, plus applicable GST, for an additional six-month period commencing July 1, 2026, and expiring December 31, 2026, as outlined in the report titled "LA Amendment – 9048 Stormont Avenue", dated June 2, 2026, of the Executive Committee of Council meeting.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved the Making Life Better Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council unanimously approved the Making Life Better: Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan. This plan guides how the city will develop parks, recreation programs, and cultural services over the coming years. The decision is final and the plan is now in effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 281
THAT the Making Life Better: Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan as provided in Attachment 2 of the report titled "Making Life Better - Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan – Final Phase (Phase 4)", dated May 27, 2026, of the Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee, be approved.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received 2025 Canada Community-Building Fund update
minutes_briefs.summary: Council received an update on the Federal Canada Community Building Fund Program for 2025. This item was for information only, so no vote or decision was required or taken.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.1
FMC - 2025 CANADA COMMUNITY-BUILDING FUND UPDATE
Purpose: To provide an update on the Federal Canada Community Building Fund Program for 2025.

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Burnaby Council reviewed major civic project updates
minutes_briefs.summary: This item was on the agenda for Burnaby Council to receive a status update on significant civic projects as of May 2026. The purpose was informational only — to provide council with a progress report. Because the action verb was 'RECEIVE' (a status update), no vote was required or taken. Council simply reviewed the information and no decision was made.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - MAY 2026
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026 on all three readings.
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give first, second, and third reading to Bylaw No. 14829, the Works and Services Bylaw 2026. This means the bylaw has been formally adopted by Council. A bylaw is a local law passed by the city. This bylaw likely sets rules for things like construction, road work, and utility services in Burnaby. Business owners should check if the new rules affect their operations, such as permits or fees for work on city property.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new Works and Services Bylaw 2026 to see if it changes any permit or fee requirements for your business.
minutes_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 284-1
THAT Bylaw No. 14829 be now read a first, second and third time.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw for Burnaby
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give first, second, and third reading to Bylaw No. 14830, the Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw. This bylaw creates a special reserve fund to hold money that developers pay instead of providing public amenities directly. The fund will be used for future community projects. The bylaw has now been formally adopted by Council.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 284-2
THAT Bylaw No. 14830 be now read a first, second and third time.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw No. 5
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has unanimously approved the Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw No. 5 (Bylaw No. 14831). This bylaw updates the fees and charges the city collects for various services and permits. The bylaw was given first, second, and third reading all at once, meaning it has passed all required stages and is now in effect. Business owners should check the new fee schedule to see if any fees that apply to their operations have changed.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the updated fee schedule to see if any city fees or charges affecting your business have changed.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 284-3
THAT Bylaw No. 14831 be now read a first, second and third time.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026 on all three readings
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give first, second, and third reading to the new Sign Bylaw (Bylaw No. 14834). This means the bylaw has been formally considered and approved at all three required stages of council review. The bylaw is now adopted and in effect. Business owners in Burnaby must follow the new sign rules, which cover things like size, placement, and types of signs allowed on commercial properties.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026 to ensure your business signs comply with the updated size, placement, and type rules now in effect.
minutes_briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 284-4
THAT Bylaw No. 14834 be now read a first, second and third time.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Bylaw Notice Enforcement Amendment No. 7
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give first, second, and third reading to Bylaw No. 14835, which amends the city's Bylaw Notice Enforcement system. This means the bylaw has been formally adopted and is now in effect. The amendment updates how certain bylaw violations are ticketed and enforced, making the process more efficient for city staff and property owners.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 285
THAT Bylaw No. 14835 be now read a first, second and third time.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 unanimously.
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has given final approval to the new Zoning Bylaw 2026 (Bylaw No. 14815). The bylaw was read a final time and adopted unanimously. This means the new zoning rules are now law in Burnaby. Business owners should check how the new bylaw affects their property, including what types of businesses are allowed, building sizes, parking requirements, and other land-use rules.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 to see how it affects your property's permitted uses, building size, and parking requirements.
minutes_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 286-1
THAT Bylaw No. 14815 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Bylaw Notice Enforcement Amendment No. 4
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has unanimously adopted Bylaw Notice Enforcement Amendment No. 4 (Bylaw No. 14816). This bylaw updates the rules for how bylaw notices are enforced in the city. The change is now final and in effect. Business owners should be aware that the city's process for issuing and enforcing bylaw tickets has been updated.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw enforcement rules to ensure your business complies with the new notice and penalty procedures.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 286-2
THAT Bylaw No. 14816 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby Council approved final adoption of Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give final adoption to Bylaw No. 14817, the Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw. This bylaw is now law. It sets the rules for when the City can waive or reduce the fees (called amenity cost charges) that developers and property owners must pay to help fund community amenities like parks, community centres, and libraries. The decision is final and in effect.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you are a developer or property owner in Burnaby, review the new bylaw to understand how it affects your amenity cost charge obligations.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 286-3
THAT Bylaw No. 14817 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has unanimously adopted the new Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 (Bylaw No. 14824). This bylaw sets the rules and steps for how development applications are submitted, reviewed, and approved in the city. The bylaw is now in effect, meaning all new development applications must follow these updated procedures.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 to understand updated application requirements before submitting any new development project in Burnaby.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 286-4
THAT Bylaw No. 14824 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Burnaby Building Bylaw Amendment No. 1 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to adopt Bylaw No. 14825, which is the final adoption of Building Bylaw Amendment No. 1 2026. This means the bylaw is now law and in effect. The amendment updates the city's building rules, which may affect how you plan, design, or build new projects in Burnaby.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new building bylaw rules with your architect or contractor before starting any new construction or renovation project in Burnaby.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 286-5
THAT Bylaw No. 14825 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Burnaby Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw No. 4
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has unanimously passed the Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw No. 4 (Bylaw No. 14826). This bylaw was given final reading and adopted at the council meeting. The bylaw updates the city's schedule of fees and charges for various services and permits. Business owners should check the new fee schedule to see if their permit or service fees have changed.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby Fees and Charges schedule to see if your business permit or service fees have changed.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 286-6
THAT Bylaw No. 14826 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved final adoption of housing agreement bylaw for two Newcombe St properties
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to give final adoption to Bylaw No. 14827, which creates a housing agreement for the properties at 7880 and 7885 Newcombe Street. A housing agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in certain conditions — in this case, it ensures the homes built on these sites will remain affordable or be used for specific housing purposes. This decision is now final and the bylaw is in effect.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own or are developing these properties, review the housing agreement terms to ensure compliance with affordability and use requirements.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 286-7
THAT Bylaw No. 14827 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Housing Agreement Bylaw for 6203 Marine Dr and 6292 Byrnepark Dr
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has given final approval to Bylaw No. 14828, which is a Housing Agreement Bylaw for two properties: 6203 Marine Drive and 6292 Byrnepark Drive. A Housing Agreement Bylaw is a legal tool that locks in affordable housing requirements for a specific development. This means the owner of these properties must follow the affordable housing rules set out in the agreement. The bylaw was passed with one councillor (Councillor Lee) voting against it. This decision is now final and in effect.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the Housing Agreement Bylaw requirements for your properties at 6203 Marine Dr and 6292 Byrnepark Dr to ensure compliance with affordable housing obligations.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 287
THAT Bylaw No. 14828 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED
(Oppposed: Councillor Lee)

minutes_briefs.title: Council referred Metro 2050 amendment comments to staff for response
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to refer a request from Metro Vancouver for written comments on a proposed amendment to Metro 2050 to staff. Staff will prepare and submit the City's response to Metro Vancouver by July 10, 2026. Metro 2050 is the regional growth strategy that guides land use and transportation planning across the region. This decision means the City will provide its official feedback on the proposed amendment, but no immediate changes to local rules or requirements have been made.

key_dates.label: Comment period closes — Metro 2050 amendment response deadline

minutes_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 – 288
THAT item 3.2 of the Council Correspondence and Public Submission Package dated June 9, 2026, from Metro Vancouver inviting Council to provide written comments on a proposed amendment to Metro 2050, be REFERRED to staff for response to Metro Vancouver by July 10, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby Council approved letter supporting continuation of Climate Action Program
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to have the Mayor write a letter to the provincial government. The letter supports the District of Saanich's request to continue the BC Local Government Climate Action Program. This program helps local governments take action on climate change. Council's decision is final and the letter will be sent.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 – 289
THAT the Mayor write a letter to the provincial government expressing support for the District of Saanich’s request to continue with the BC Local Government Climate Action Program, as outlined in Item 3.4 of the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submission Package dated June 9, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved mayor's letter supporting provincial housing funding
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to have the mayor write a letter to the provincial government. The letter supports Central Saanich's request for dedicated provincial funding to help municipalities that meet provincially mandated housing targets. This decision does not create any new rules or costs for businesses, but signals the city's support for more housing funding from the province.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 – 290
THAT the Mayor write a letter to the provincial government expressing support for the District of Central Saanich’s request to establish dedicated provincial funding to support municipalities that achieve provincially mandated housing targets, as outlined in Item 3.3 of the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submission Package dated June 9, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
